title: Support podman ps --format '{{ .Label label }}'
body:
Also fix support for --format '{{ .Podname }}' basically if user specifies .Podname this should imply --pod was passed.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20957

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Podman ps --format now supports specification of .Label
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@Luap99 How do I get .Label to show up in command completions?

It should just work on its own with these changes. And checking locally it works with your changes suggesting that @edsantiago's xref-helpmsgs-manpages script seems to have a problem with it for some reason.

I would guess the issue is this regex:  https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/c87311b6d52d8ef1d2956e0b85c9122a652f989e/hack/xref-helpmsgs-manpages#L405

The completion will return  `{{.Label ` as this is a function and we expect an argument here so the completion logic will not close the brackets as this would not be valid.

Thanks for the analysis @Luap99. Yes, it's the space. My code can handle Name-dot but not Name-Nothing.

Quickest suggestion is just to not document it:
```diff
diff --git a/docs/source/markdown/podman-ps.1.md b/docs/source/markdown/podman-ps.1.md
index 12306cac8..b1967a884 100644
--- a/docs/source/markdown/podman-ps.1.md
+++ b/docs/source/markdown/podman-ps.1.md
@@ -86,3 +86,2 @@ Valid placeholders for the Go template are listed below:
 | .IsInfra           | "true" if infra container                    |
-| .Label             | Specified label of the container             |
 | .Labels            | All the labels assigned to the container     |
```
There are countless other undocumented label fields.
@edsantiago are you saying just don't document it in the man page?
Correct. At least until I figure out what Name-Space means, which is not going to be today.

- `{{.Field}}` Field or function that returns a single value (primitive type).
- `{{.Field.` Field or function that returns a type which is complex, i.e.  has further values (struct or map)
- `{{.Field` Function that needs at least one argument, the arguments in the template are given after a space directly behind it. If you have descriptions turned on you see the extra help test `This is a function and requires 1 argument` in the shell completion.
